channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇬🇧🇨🇳 British MPs to Visit China for First Time in Seven Years
item.description: A cross-party delegation of UK lawmakers will visit China this month for the first time since 2019, signaling warming ties following Prime Minister Keir Starmer's January visit.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪⚔️ Trump Announces Withdrawal of 5,000 US Troops from Germany
item.description: President Trump said he will withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany as tensions with NATO allies widen over the Iran war, raising questions about the future of the transatlantic alliance.

item.title: 🇨🇳💰 China Export Growth Quickens on Iran War Stockpiling
item.description: China's export growth likely accelerated in April as companies rushed to stockpile components amid fears the Iran war could push input costs higher, a Reuters poll shows.

item.title: 🇬🇧🗳️ UK Labour Suffers Heavy Losses as Reform UK Makes Gains
item.description: Partial results from English local elections show large losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour Party and gains for the hard-right Reform UK party.

item.title: 🌏⚖️ ASEAN Leaders Meet on Iran War Economic Fallout
item.description: Southeast Asian leaders are meeting under pressure to mitigate the impact on their economies from the Iran war, with one minister saying it "should not have occurred in the first place."

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺⚔️ Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of violating Victory Day ceasefire
item.description: Russia and Ukraine traded accusations of violating a two-day ceasefire announced by Moscow to cover Soviet Victory Day celebrations. The fragile truce failed to hold as both sides reported attacks along the frontlines.

item.title: 🇰🇵🇰🇷💥 North Korea announces new long-range artillery systems targeting Seoul
item.description: North Korea says it will deploy new long-range artillery systems capable of striking the Seoul metropolitan area. The announcement comes amid continued military cooperation with Russia and tensions on the Korean peninsula.

item.title: 🇵🇾🇹🇼 Paraguay reaffirms Taiwan ties as China pressures last South American ally
item.description: Paraguay President Santiago Pena visited Taipei to reaffirm diplomatic support for Taiwan, one of only 12 countries maintaining formal relations. China has intensified efforts to draw Paraguay away, with lawmakers visiting Beijing amid internal debate over economic costs.

item.title: 🇦🇲🇺🇦 Armenia distances from Russia as Pashinyan says "not an ally" on Ukraine
item.description: Armenian PM Nikol Pashinyan clarified Armenia is not allied with Russia on Ukraine, confirming he will skip Moscow Victory Day parade. The Kremlin summoned Armenia's ambassador after Zelensky's visit to Yerevan, showing tensions as Armenia drifts toward EU.

item.title: 🚢🛢️ Maersk warns energy crunch to persist even if Iran peace deal struck
item.description: Shipping giant Maersk said the Iran war has pushed fuel costs up nearly $500 million a month, with the energy crisis set to continue even if a peace deal is reached. The warning came as oil supplies tighten further with depleted storage tanks meeting peak summer demand.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷⚔️ US and Iran Exchange Fire as Fragile Ceasefire Shows Cracks
item.description: US military carried out retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets after renewed hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz. The UAE reported intercepting drones and missiles as Trump insisted the ceasefire still holds.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧⚔️ Netanyahu Vows No Immunity for Enemies After Beirut Strike
item.description: Israel struck a Hezbollah commander in Beirut, its first strike on the capital since the ceasefire. Netanyahu declared there would be no immunity for militants threatening Israel.

item.title: 🇺🇸🌍📉 Global Views of US Fall Below Russia Under Trump, Survey Finds
item.description: An annual democracy study shows US global perceptions have deteriorated for a second year and now rank worse than Russia, as Trump’s policies strain the NATO alliance.

item.title: 🇧🇷🇺🇸🏛️ Lula Visits Trump Seeking to Avert Trade Tariffs
item.description: Brazilian President Lula met with Trump in Washington to discuss trade relations and tariffs. The two leaders skipped a joint press appearance despite signs of thaw in relations.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia ramps up assassination campaign against opponents abroad, Western intelligence says
item.description: Western intelligence officials report Russia has intensified targeted killings abroad since the 2022 Ukraine invasion, going after Russian activists and foreign supporters of Ukraine using recruited proxies. Plots have been disrupted in France, Germany, Lithuania, and Poland.

item.title: 📉 US stocks fall from records as oil prices yo-yo amid Iran tensions
item.description: US stocks dropped from their record highs while oil prices fluctuated sharply as tensions between the US and Iran continued to roil global markets.

item.title: 🇹🇷🇮🇱 Hamas Operating Training Camps in Turkey for Attacks on Israel
item.description: Kan 11 News reports Hamas is using Turkish shooting ranges and drone schools to train operatives for future conflicts with Israel, raising tensions between Israel and Turkey.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇵🇰🇮🇳 China to Supply J-35 Stealth Jets to Pakistan, Edge Over India
item.description: Chinese J-35 stealth fighter jets are set to give Pakistan an advantage over India in fifth-generation aircraft technology, as India lags in acquiring stealth capabilities.

item.title: 🇦🇪💥 UAE Air Defenses Respond to Missile Threat as Regional Tensions Escalate
item.description: UAE authorities activated air defenses in response to a missile threat amid ongoing US-Iran hostilities, urging residents to follow official channels for safety updates.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱🇺🇸 Third Round of Lebanon-Israel Direct Talks Set for Next Week in Washington
item.description: Lebanon and Israel will hold their third round of direct negotiations in Washington next week, mediated by the US State Department, continuing unprecedented diplomatic engagement.

item.title: 🇺🇳 US-Iran resolution at UN faces Chinese and Russian vetoes
item.description: The US urged countries to support a UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks on the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say China and Russia are likely to veto it, highlighting geopolitical divisions over the conflict.

item.title: 🎨 Pussy Riot protests Russia return to Venice Biennale
item.description: Dozens of activists from the anti-Kremlin art collective Pussy Riot demonstrated in Venice, waving Ukrainian flags and chanting against Putin to oppose Moscow presence at the Venice Biennale for the first time since the 2022 invasion.

item.title: 🇧🇷 Brazil Lula reports progress after Trump White House meeting
item.description: Brazilian President Lula reported progress in relations with the US after a three-hour meeting with Trump in Washington, seeking to avert new trade tariffs that had strained bilateral ties.

item.title: 🇷🇸 Serbia rejects local bid for Russian-owned NIS oil firm
item.description: Serbias President Vucic dismissed a bid by a recently formed local company to purchase a majority stake in the Russian-owned NIS oil firm, citing lack of expertise amid ongoing talks with Hungarian MOL.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil prices jump as US-Iran hostilities threaten ceasefire
item.description: Oil prices rose more than 1% after renewed fighting between the US and Iran threatened a shaky ceasefire and dashed hopes for progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a key transit route.
